label: VALID_SIGNAL
definition: Recovered heuristic: the span is treated as a strong prompt signal when it appears inside an S2-4a evidence-pack block with explicit formulation-relevant content such as preparation steps, formulation/result table summaries, or explicit optimization conclusions, and it does not show obvious surface-noise markers.
implementation_location: Upstream prompt producer is maintained at src/stage2_sampling_labels/run_stage2_s2_4a_prompt_construction_v1.py and src/stage2_sampling_labels/extract_semantic_stage2_objects_v2.py::build_live_prompt; the VALID_SIGNAL labeling implementation itself was not found in maintained source and is recoverable only from data/frozen/dev15_stage2_freeze_v1/s2_4a/analysis/s2_4a_prompt_noise_examples.md plus s2_4a_prompt_noise_metrics.tsv

label: WEAK_SIGNAL
definition: Recovered heuristic catch-all: the span is not obvious surface noise but also is not a clean, direct formulation-defining signal; mixed-purpose materials, context, background, partially relevant result prose, and contaminated-but-still-relevant blocks appear to fall here by default.
implementation_location: No maintained source file or function defining WEAK_SIGNAL was found by repository search; the rule is inferred from the frozen prompt-noise artifacts, especially the residual-ratio behavior in data/frozen/dev15_stage2_freeze_v1/s2_4a/analysis/s2_4a_prompt_noise_metrics.tsv and the example framing in s2_4a_prompt_noise_examples.md

label: NOISE
definition: Recovered heuristic: the span is treated as prompt noise when it matches obvious surface artifacts such as author and affiliation strings, title or abstract bleed, reference-tail content, corrupted parser fragments, duplicate payloads, or suspicious tail sections; this is pattern and position driven rather than semantic understanding.
implementation_location: No maintained source file or function defining NOISE for the S2-4a prompt-noise audit was found; the rule is inferred from data/frozen/dev15_stage2_freeze_v1/s2_4a/analysis/s2_4a_prompt_noise_examples.md, the duplicate_block_count and reference_section_detected fields in s2_4a_prompt_noise_metrics.tsv, and the frozen prompts in s2_4a_prompts_v1.jsonl
